Today is February 17, 2026. Civil rights leader Jesse Jackson recently passed away at the age of 84. According to reports on his health history, what was his final medical diagnosis regarding his degenerative brain disease?
(A) Progressive supranuclear palsy
(B) Parkinson's disease
(C) Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS)
(D) Alzheimer's disease
(E) Huntington's disease
explanation: The article states that Jackson was diagnosed with Parkinson's disease in 2017, but that diagnosis was "subsequently changed to one of progressive supranuclear palsy."
model_output:
Based on recent reports, Jesse Jackson was initially diagnosed with Parkinson's disease in 2017, but this was updated in April 2025 to progressive supranuclear palsy (PSP), which was his final diagnosis for the degenerative brain disease. He was hospitalized in November 2025 amid complications from PSP, and his recent death at age 84 has been linked to that condition.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [WSBTV] (https://www.wsbtv.com/news/trending/psp-vs-parkinsons-understanding-differences-symptoms-treatment/4VNCWZUBUBHDRMAS2F3KHKGKOY)[[1]](https://www.wsbtv.com/news/trending/psp-vs-parkinsons-understanding-differences-symptoms-treatment/4VNCWZUBUBHDRMAS2F3KHKGKOY)
- [NBC News] (https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/jesse-jackson-hospitalized-neurodegenerative-condition-rcna243755)[[2]](https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/jesse-jackson-hospitalized-neurodegenerative-condition-rcna243755)
- [ABC7] (https://abc7.com/post/civil-rights-activist-rev-jesse-jackson-hospitalized-amid-progressive-supranuclear-palsy-psp-diagnosis-sources-say/18151405)[[3]](https://abc7.com/post/civil-rights-activist-rev-jesse-jackson-hospitalized-amid-progressive-supranuclear-palsy-psp-diagnosis-sources-say/18151405)
- [CurePSP] (https://www.psp.org/news/details/curepsp-stands-with-rev-jesse-l-jackson-sr-amid-hospitalization-with-progressive-supranuclear-palsy-psp)[[4]](https://www.psp.org/news/details/curepsp-stands-with-rev-jesse-l-jackson-sr-amid-hospitalization-with-progressive-supranuclear-palsy-psp)

Today is February 17, 2026. US Senator Mark Kelly has indicated he will "seriously consider" a 2028 presidential run. Which specific academic or professional distinction did he cite as making him "very different from most senators"?
(A) He is the only former Navy captain currently serving in the Senate.
(B) He is the first former astronaut to serve as a Senator from Arizona.
(C) He is the only person in the U.S. Senate with a graduate degree in engineering.
(D) He is the only Senator to have spent exactly 25 years in the military.
(E) He is the only Senator whose spouse survived an assassination attempt.
explanation: Mark Kelly stated, "I'm one of the very few engineers, I'm the only person with a graduate degree in engineering in the United States Senate."
model_output:
First, a quick note: The date is given as February 17, 2026, which aligns with recent reports about Sen. Kelly's statements on a potential 2028 run.

Based on recent reporting about Kelly's comments (from a BBC interview), he cited multiple aspects of his background as setting him apart, including his engineering background, combat experience, and time in the military. However, the *specific* distinction that matches one of the options—and that he explicitly called out as unique to him in the current Senate—is being the only sitting senator with a graduate degree in engineering.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [BBC News] (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cy9gdvl4zv4o)[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cy9gdvl4zv4o)

Today is February 17, 2026. In the ongoing investigation into the kidnapping of Nancy Guthrie, police recently analyzed a glove found near the scene. Where specifically was this glove recovered?
(A) Inside a Walmart store near the backpack display
(B) On the floor of Nancy Guthrie's front porch
(C) In the backyard of her daughter Annie Guthrie's house
(D) In a field near the side of the road about two miles from her home
(E) In a trash bin outside a church in Pima County
explanation: The article states the FBI recovered the glove "from a field near the side of the road about two miles (3 km) from Guthrie's home near Tucson, Arizona."
model_output:
Based on web searches related to the Nancy Guthrie kidnapping case, multiple sources indicate that a black glove was recovered approximately two miles from her home in Tucson, Arizona. One report specifically notes it was "found in a field a few miles from Guthrie's home"[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cj0dqp9pleeo), while another describes it as being "found on the roadside in Nancy Guthrie's neighborhood 1.5 miles from the house"[[2]](https://www.facebook.com/NickCiletti/posts/just-in-dna-evidence-was-found-on-a-glove-that-was-discovered-about-two-miles-aw/1396660362258551). This aligns closely with option (D), and no sources mentioned details matching the other options.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- BBC ([https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cj0dqp9pleeo])
- Facebook ([https://www.facebook.com/NickCiletti/posts/just-in-dna-evidence-was-found-on-a-glove-that-was-discovered-about-two-miles-aw/1396660362258551])

Today is February 17, 2026. Hollywood is mourning the death of Robert Duvall at age 95. For which specific role did Duvall win his Academy Award for Best Actor in 1983?
(A) Tom Hagen in The Godfather
(B) Lieutenant Colonel William Kilgore in Apocalypse Now
(C) Mac Sledge in Tender Mercies
(D) Augustus McCrae in Lonesome Dove
(E) Boo Radley in To Kill a Mockingbird
explanation: The article states: "Duvall won the Oscar for best actor in 1983 for playing a washed-up country singer in Tender Mercies."
model_output:
Robert Duvall won the Academy Award for Best Actor in 1983 for his role as Mac Sledge in the film *Tender Mercies*[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robert_Duvall)[[2]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cv2grggdzweo)[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Robert_Duvall). This is confirmed across multiple sources, and it's distinct from his other notable roles, many of which earned him nominations in supporting categories or for television (like *Lonesome Dove*). The query's mention of his passing at age 95 aligns with recent reports as of February 17, 2026[[4]](https://www.al.com/life/2026/02/legendary-actor-from-the-godfather-dead-at-95-lasting-and-unforgettable.html)[[5]](https://www.wdbj7.com/2026/02/16/actor-robert-duvall-dead-95-wife-confirms).

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

- BBC ([https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cv2grggdzweo])

Today is February 17, 2026. Former President Barack Obama recently clarified his comments regarding the existence of aliens. What was the primary point of his clarification posted on Instagram?
(A) That he has personally seen classified footage of alien specimens in Area 51.
(B) That there is an underground facility for alien research that was hidden from him.
(C) That he was joking during the podcast and does not believe life exists outside Earth.
(D) That the chances of Earth being visited are low and he saw no evidence of contact while president.
(E) That the "lightning round" format forced him to lie about the existence of UFOs.
explanation: Obama clarified that while the universe is vast and life is likely out there, "the chances we've been visited by aliens is low, and I saw no evidence during my presidency that extraterrestrials have made contact with us."
model_output:
Based on the clarification posted by Barack Obama on Instagram, the primary point was that while he believes extraterrestrial life is statistically likely somewhere in the universe, the chances of Earth having been visited by aliens are low, and he saw no evidence of any extraterrestrial contact during his presidency[[1]](https://www.instagram.com/reel/DUy9E_UD9RR?hl=en). This aligns directly with option (D), as it emphasizes the low probability of visits and lack of evidence for contact (rather than denying the existence of alien life entirely or claiming to have seen evidence).

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Instagram] (https://www.instagram.com/reel/DUy9E_UD9RR?hl=en)

Today is February 17, 2026. Investigators searching for Nancy Guthrie are reportedly using a new technological strategy to locate her. What specific device is being used in this effort?
(A) A thermal imaging camera mounted on a drone
(B) A ground-penetrating radar system
(C) A facial recognition AI linked to Walmart surveillance
(D) A satellite-based GPS tracker for her mobile phone
(E) A "signal sniffer" designed to detect her pacemaker
explanation: The article states: "They are using a device that could detect signals coming from her pacemaker... the tool, called a 'signal sniffer', was mounted on a helicopter."
model_output:
Nancy Guthrie, a missing person from Tucson, Arizona, has been the subject of an ongoing search as of February 17, 2026. Based on recent news reports, investigators (including the FBI) are using advanced technology to try locating her, specifically by attempting to detect signals from her pacemaker.

This aligns directly with option (E), which describes a "signal sniffer" device designed for that purpose. Other options like thermal imaging, radar, facial recognition, or GPS tracking don't match the reported strategy.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

(A) Double-touching
(B) Sweeping past the tee line
(C) Using an illegal broom head
(D) Exceeding the delivery time limit
(E) Intentional stone displacement
explanation: The article explains the controversy centers on "double-touching" (or "double-tapping"), which is "touching the stone a second time after initially releasing it down the ice."
model_output:
Based on recent reports from the 2026 Winter Games, Canadian curlers Marc Kennedy and Rachel Homan have been accused of "double-touching" the stone, which involves illegally touching it a second time after release while it's in motion[[1]](https://www.cbc.ca/sports/olympics/winter/curling/curling-double-touch-controversy-canada-9.7092482). This appears to be the specific technical violation in question, as multiple sources confirm the allegations centered on this rule breach during matches[[2]](https://www.npr.org/2026/02/16/nx-s1-5715941/a-curling-scandal-rocks-olympic-ice)[[3]](https://sports.yahoo.com/olympics/article/winter-olympics-2026-canada-womens-curling-team-accused-of-violating-rules-after-men-face-same-allegations-152837120.html).

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

Today is February 17, 2026. In a retrospective of Robert Duvall's life, the actor's early years in New York are highlighted. Which two fellow actors were his classmates and lifelong friends during that time?
(A) Al Pacino and Robert De Niro
(B) Dustin Hoffman and Gene Hackman
(C) Marlon Brando and James Caan
(D) Michael Caine and Laurence Olivier
(E) Kevin Costner and Tom Cruise
explanation: The article states: "His classmates included Dustin Hoffman and Gene Hackman, who both became lifelong friends."
model_output:
Robert Duvall studied acting at the Neighborhood Playhouse School of the Theatre in New York City under Sanford Meisner, where his classmates and close friends included Dustin Hoffman and Gene Hackman[[1]](https://parade.com/news/robert-duvall-school-dustin-hoffman-gene-hackman-nyc)[[2]](https://www.rogerebert.com/tributes/robert-duvall-tribute-2026). The three were struggling actors who even roomed together and supported each other in their early careers, forming lifelong bonds[[3]](https://www.reddit.com/r/todayilearned/comments/1dwnn8h/til_dustin_hoffman_gene_hackman_and_robert_duvall)[[4]](https://www.californiasun.co/the-three-musketeers-of-a-sort-duvall-hackman-and-hoffman). None of the other options align with this timeline or historical record of his early New York years (e.g., the actors in A, C, and E are from different generations, while those in D were primarily based in the UK).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Robert Duvall Attended School With Dustin Hoffman and Gene ... (https://parade.com/news/robert-duvall-school-dustin-hoffman-gene-hackman-nyc)
- [Robert] Duvall subsequently moved to New York to study acting ... (https://www.facebook.com/FolliesofGod/posts/robert-duvall-subsequently-moved-to-new-york-to-study-acting-working-as-a-postal/1456336083170129)
- TIL Dustin Hoffman, Gene Hackman and Robert Duvall were ... (https://www.reddit.com/r/todayilearned/comments/1dwnn8h/til_dustin_hoffman_gene_hackman_and_robert_duvall)
- The three musketeers, of a sort: Duvall, Hackman, and Hoffman (https://www.californiasun.co/the-three-musketeers-of-a-sort-duvall-hackman-and-hoffman)
- Something Lasting and Unforgettable: Robert Duvall (1931-2026) (https://www.rogerebert.com/tributes/robert-duvall-tribute-2026)

(B) It encouraged military personnel to refuse illegal orders.
(C) It leaked classified intelligence regarding narco-trafficking boats.
(D) It criticized the Pentagon's decision to demote Kelly's rank.
(E) It called for the impeachment of the Secretary of State.
explanation: The article states the video was "encouraging troops to disobey orders they deemed illegal."
model_output:
The search results confirm that the 90-second video, published in November 2025 by Senator Mark Kelly and five other Democratic lawmakers (all with military or national security backgrounds), urged U.S. military personnel to uphold the Constitution by refusing to follow illegal orders. This drew backlash from the Trump administration, leading to investigations, attempted indictments, and efforts to punish Kelly (including a potential demotion of his Navy reserve rank). A grand jury ultimately declined to indict them, and a judge blocked punitive actions by the Pentagon.

This directly matches option (B). The other options do not align with the reported content of the video.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 17, 2026. Following the death of Robert Duvall, many of his iconic roles are being remembered. Which role did Duvall himself often cite as his personal favorite?
(A) Tom Hagen in The Godfather
(B) Augustus McCrae in Lonesome Dove
(C) The evangelical preacher in The Apostle
(D) Boo Radley in To Kill a Mockingbird
(E) The corporate executive in Network
explanation: The article states: "Duvall often said his favourite role was as the Texas Ranger-turned-cowboy Augustus McCrae in Lonesome Dove."
model_output:
Based on web search results from multiple sources, including recent obituaries and tributes following Robert Duvall's death (which apparently occurred shortly before February 17, 2026, based on the query context and search hits), Duvall consistently cited his role as Augustus "Gus" McCrae in the 1989 miniseries *Lonesome Dove* as his personal favorite. For example, a Hollywood Reporter obituary explicitly states: "in a lifetime of great roles, Duvall's favorite was playing ex-Texas Ranger Augustus McCrae in the 1989 CBS miniseries Lonesome Dove"[[1]](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/robert-duvall-dead-godfather-apocalypse-now-1236506861). Other sources, such as Men's Journal and various social media posts, echo this sentiment, with Duvall himself describing the character as "like Shakespeare" and the project as one of the highlights of his life[[2]](https://www.mensjournal.com/entertainment/robert-duvall-favorite-role)[[3]](https://www.facebook.com/oastories/videos/%F0%9D%90%91%F0%9D%90%88%F0%9D%90%8F-%F0%9D%90%91%F0%9D%90%A8%F0%9D%90%9B%F0%9D%90%9E%F0%9D%90%AB%F0%9D%90%AD-%F0%9D%90%83%F0%9D%90%AE%F0%9D%90%95%F0%9D%90%9A%F0%9D%90%A5%F0%9D%90%A5%F0%9D%90%AC-%F0%9D%90%85%F0%9D%90%9A%F0%9D%90%AF%F0%9D%90%A8%F0%9D%90%AB%F0%9D%90%A2%F0%9D%90%AD%F0%9D%90%9E-%F0%9D%90%91%F0%9D%90%A8%F0%9D%90%A5%F0%9D%90%9E-%F0%9D%90%86%F0%9D%90%AE%F0%9D%90%AC-%F0%9D%90%8C%F0%9D%90%9C%F0%9D%90%82%F0%9D%90%AB%F0%9D%90%9A%F0%9D%90%B2-%F0%9D%90%A2%F0%9D%90%A7-%F0%9D%90%8B%F0%9D%90%A8%F0%9D%90%A7%F0%9D%90%9E%F0%9D%90%AC%F0%9D%90%A8%F0%9D%90%A6%F0%9D%90%9E-%F0%9D%90%83%F0%9D%90%A8%F0%9D%90%AF%F0%9D%90%9E/3009365065913738)[[4]](https://www.aol.com/lifestyle/robert-duvalls-favorite-role-behind-215244777.html). While he was also very proud of roles like the preacher in *The Apostle* (which he wrote and directed), the consensus from Duvall's own statements points to *Lonesome Dove* as the top pick.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

- Hollywood Reporter (https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/robert-duvall-dead-godfather-apocalypse-now-1236506861)

explanation: Jackson is quoted saying: "Ten to 15 years ago, Venezuela was producing 1.5 million barrels a day more than it does today."
model_output:
Based on recent statements attributed to William Jackson of Capital Economics in multiple sources, Venezuela's current oil production is down by 1.5 million barrels per day compared to 10-15 years ago[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c4gjx1j1nkjo)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/trump-big-plans-venezuelas-oil-002936515.html).

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 17, 2026. Jesse Jackson's 1988 speech to the Democratic National Convention is remembered for a specific refrain that was later echoed by Barack Obama. What was that refrain?
(A) "Hope and change"
(B) "Yes we can"
(C) "The rainbow coalition"
(D) "A new ball game"
(E) "Keep hope alive"
explanation: The article states: "His speech to the 1988 Democratic National Convention, which ended with the refrain 'keep hope alive', would be echoed decades later..."
model_output:
First, a quick note: It appears from search results that Rev. Jesse Jackson passed away today (February 17, 2026). He was a civil rights giant, and his legacy is immense.

Based on reviewing transcripts and analyses of Jackson's famous 1988 DNC speech, the key refrain was "Keep hope alive!"—repeated emphatically at the end. This was later echoed in Barack Obama's 2008 presidential campaign, particularly through his "hope and change" slogan and overall theme of hope.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>
